engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is deciding whether to trust a financial-advisory firm with their money — sells defined expertise (life planning, tax, investing) backed by credentials (RIA/SEC), a decade of history, and named client testimonials. That is the top rung of the ladder.

goal.one_action: book/request a consultation call with an advisor

structure.archetype_note: Classic multi-page advisory site: home pitch → for-clients services → about/leadership → dedicated contact/booking page with Calendly embed. Also a for-advisors audience split + resources/blog.

mapped.observed_as: Money should feel empowering, not overwhelming.
Talk to an Advisor / Book a call

confidence.why: fetch_quality full; clear multi-page advisory trust site with unambiguous single conversion action (book a call). Minor gaps (stat numbers, Calendly field list) noted in oddities but do not affect classification.

gap_findings: Booking here is a single-step Calendly embed (no inventory/multi-step) — correctly treated as a delegated form mechanism, not a booking-* capability, per the coltivar precedent.
Dual-audience nav (For Clients / For Advisors) — the taxonomy has no explicit way to express a secondary B2B audience split, but it did not force an unmapped section here.
